_provenance: Hand-derived from Sprint 3 Overfitting Conscience design (team-lead dispatch brief). Scenario inputs are plain integers and enum strings. No producer-computed rates or percents are pinned — verdicts are enum-constrained (CLEAR/WATCH/BREACH), ratios are derived from fixture inputs. All spec_bundle_ids are synthetic hashes for isolation.
_design_refs: Sprint-3 Overfitting Conscience dispatch brief — indicator definitions
NN1 multiple-testing haircut (researcher_dof_ledger BACKTEST_SELECTION S accumulator)

honest_s0_clear._description: NN1-honest case: no BACKTEST_SELECTION rows for the bundle. S=0, verdict=CLEAR.
honest_s0_clear._note: raw_response must note 'no BACKTEST_SELECTION facets' and that N_effective equals N_optuna

s_gt_0_low_ratio_watch._description: Indicator 1: S > 0 but S/N_optuna ratio <= 0.1. verdict=WATCH (uncounted testing present but ratio below BREACH threshold).

ledger_rows._note: S=5, N_optuna inferred as 500; ratio=0.01 well below 0.1 BREACH threshold

s_gt_0_low_ratio_watch._note: raw_response must include BACKTEST_SELECTION facet count and names

ledger_rows._note: ratio = 60/500 = 0.12 which exceeds the 0.1 BREACH threshold

high_ratio_breach._note: raw_response must include ratio and threshold

different_bundle_excluded._description: Ledger row pointing at a different spec_bundle_id must be excluded — only rows matching the autotune_run's bundle are in scope.

ledger_rows._note: Points at a DIFFERENT bundle — must not contribute to S for this run

different_bundle_excluded._note: Cross-bundle ledger rows must not pollute this run's S count

ratio_boundary_exactly_0_1_is_watch._description: Boundary: S/N_optuna = 0.1 exactly (50/500). The threshold is 'S/N_optuna > 0.1', so 0.1 exactly is NOT a breach — verdict is WATCH (Indicator 1: S>0) not BREACH (Indicator 2 requires strict >).
ratio_boundary_exactly_0_1_is_watch._boundary_clarification: Indicator 2 BREACH condition is STRICT: ratio > 0.1, so 0.1 exactly = NOT a breach. If S>0, verdict is WATCH (Indicator 1 fires), not BREACH.
ratio_boundary_exactly_0_1_is_watch._note: S/N_optuna = 0.1 exactly triggers WATCH (Indicator 1: S > 0) but NOT BREACH (Indicator 2: strict >)

prior_runs_all_none_s_count._description: I-3 None-guard: prior_runs where every s_count is None (pre-migration rows). Must NOT raise TypeError. Expected: no drift detected, verdict determined by I-1/I-2 only.
prior_runs_all_none_s_count._provenance: Hand-derived. Reproduces the live crash: '<' not supported between instances of NoneType and NoneType at I-3 comparison.
prior_runs_all_none_s_count._note: S=2 fires I-1 → WATCH. Prior None rows must not crash the drift check. No drift detected because non-None run count < 2.

prior_runs_mixed_none_and_int_s_count._description: I-3 None-guard: prior_runs with a mix of None and valid int s_counts. The None entries must be dropped before the monotonicity check. With one valid prior run + current, that is < 2 valid prior runs — insufficient for drift detection.
prior_runs_mixed_none_and_int_s_count._provenance: Hand-derived. Tests that None-dropping logic discards nulls and also requires >= 2 valid prior run rows.
prior_runs_mixed_none_and_int_s_count._note: After dropping Nones: one valid prior (s=1) + current (s=3). Only 1 valid prior run remains — insufficient for monotonicity check (need >= 2 prior runs). No drift detected. S=3 fires I-1 → WATCH.

prior_runs_none_mixed_sufficient_for_dri._description: I-3 None-guard + drift preserved: prior_runs with some Nones but enough non-None rows after dropping to still detect monotonic drift. Three valid prior runs with increasing S + None interleaved.
prior_runs_none_mixed_sufficient_for_dri._provenance: Hand-derived. Ensures None-dropping does NOT silently suppress a real drift signal when enough valid rows remain.
prior_runs_none_mixed_sufficient_for_dri._note: After dropping Nones: [2, 4] + current [7] = [2, 4, 7] strictly increasing → drift detected. S=7 fires I-1 and I-3 → WATCH.

prior_runs_single_none_entry._description: Edge: exactly one prior_run entry and its s_count is None. After dropping: zero valid prior runs — no drift possible. Must not crash.
prior_runs_single_none_entry._provenance: Hand-derived. Tests the len < 2 guard with a single None entry.
prior_runs_single_none_entry._note: Single None prior run → 0 valid prior rows → no drift. S=1 fires I-1 → WATCH.
